steps.title: Describe the accessory and key details
steps.description: Share what the accessory is (earrings, necklaces, bracelets, hair accessories, etc.), its material, and what you want viewers to notice most.

steps.title: Define model styling and scene
steps.description: Specify model vibe (minimal, editorial, everyday), framing (close-up, half-body), and environment (studio, street, lifestyle) to align with your brand.

steps.title: Generate AI accessories model displays
steps.description: The workflow creates model-focused visuals that showcase how accessories sit and read in context, ready for use across product and campaign assets.

steps.title: Curate, review, and export
steps.description: Select images that best reflect your brand and product promise, then export for PDPs, collection pages, and social media placements.

items.question: What is the Accessories Model Display For E-Commerce workflow?
items.answer: It is an AI workflow that generates on-model visuals for accessories, helping brands present jewelry and accessories on styled models for e-commerce and campaigns.

items.question: What inputs should I provide?
items.answer: Describe the accessory type, material, target audience, and preferred styling approach. Adding reference imagery or mood cues can further guide the look.

items.question: Can I control how close-up the shots are?
items.answer: Yes. You can request tight close-ups for detail, mid-shots for context, or a mix, and iterate based on what works best for your product pages.

items.question: Where can I use the generated assets?
items.answer: Use them in PDP galleries, collection overviews, hero sections, social posts, and performance creatives that feature accessories.

items.question: Does this replace traditional model photoshoots?
items.answer: It’s a flexible complement—especially useful for smaller launches, content refreshes, or SKUs that don’t justify full shoots.

items.question: How does pricing work?
items.answer: Each run consumes credits based on underlying generation steps and is structured so you can explore multiple looks per accessory.
